raw_consumer_statement:
In XX/XX/XXXX, I placed Wayfair Order # XXXX with a Wayfair credit card, which included XXXX sets of barstools and rugs totaling {$1100.00}. XXXX rug ( {$310.00} ) was unavailable, bringing the charged amount to {$860.00}. XXXX set of barstools ( {$230.00} ) was never receivedinstead, I was sent a random set of dining chairs. 

I contacted Wayfair, submitted photo evidence, and was told the incorrect item could not be exchanged. I was instructed to dispose of the dining chairs and was informed on XX/XX/XXXX that a refund of {$230.00} would be issued. 

I never received this refundno mailed check, no credit, no gift card. On XX/XX/XXXX, I spent time on the phone with both Wayfair and Citi to resolve it, and was told it would be escalated. I received a letter from Citi dated XX/XX/XXXX stating a refund had been sent, but no proof was provided and no refund was ever received. 

I repurchased the correct barstools separately and am still being charged for the original incorrect item. I submitted a dispute and a follow-up, but have received no resolution or response since XXXX. I am now being held financially responsible for a product I did not receive and was explicitly told to discard. 

I am requesting that the {$230.00} charge be removed from my account and that proof of refund issuance ( date, method, transaction ID ) be provided if Citi/Wayfair still claim it was sent.
